content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

All three amendments identify real issues. The code still has a diagnostics mismatch between the singular `final_reviewer` role and the actual `final_review.backends` panel, the tracked diff does not implement any backend-default logic changes in source, and the checked-in UTM run-state now records a reviewer pair that is inconsistent with the tracked prompt/config defaults.

## Votes

- `fr-3-9c6f07f2`: Accept
  Verified in source and by running the CLI. The compiled final-review panel defaults come from `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:259-271` and `:1504-1508`, and `resolve_final_review_panel()` uses that backend list in `src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs:128-158`. But `show_effective()` still iterates `BackendPolicyRole::ALL` and resolves `FinalReviewer` through `resolve_role_target()` (`src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:785-829`, `src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs:60-83`), which falls back to planner-family logic instead of the explicit final-review panel members. In a fresh workspace, `backend show-effective --json` reports only `final_reviewer = claude/claude-opus-4-6`, while `backend probe --role final_review_panel --flow standard --json` reports the actual panel `codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh` plus `claude/claude-opus-4-6`. That contradiction is a real diagnostics bug.
- `fr-3-9c639dfb`: Accept
  The tracked patch does not modify the implementation files named in the amendment. `git diff --stat` and the file diff show tracked changes only in `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/journal.ndjson` and `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/run.json`; `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs` and `src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs` are unchanged. If the requested work is to change backend defaults, updating only run-state artifacts does not implement that behavior in code.
- `fr-3-87665d73`: Accept
  The tracked state files do record a two-Codex reviewer panel: `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/run.json:19-27` and `:55-63` store `gpt-5.4-xhigh` plus `gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh`, and `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/journal.ndjson:42-43` records the same pair at proposal start. That conflicts with the tracked task prompt, which requires default reviewers `Codex gpt-5.4 xhigh + Opus 4.6` in `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/prompt.md`, and the tracked project config does not declare a two-Codex reviewer override in `.ralph-burning/projects/utm/config.toml`. Because `RunSnapshot` is canonical persisted run state (`src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:80-104`) and final-review reviewer snapshots participate in resume/drift state (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8213-8229`), checking in a mismatched reviewer panel is unsafe and misleading even if some resume paths may later re-resolve it.
